project.name: ree-v3
project.description: Active V3 implementation substrate for the REE cognitive-architecture research program: biologically-structured PyTorch/NumPy brain-region modules (predictors, amygdala, PFC, hippocampal, cingulate, policy, sleep, residue) integrated by a central agent orchestrator, plus a stdlib-HTTP coordinator service (SQLite + phase3 git-sync writers) that dispatches experiments to a worker fleet.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: Pure-stdlib (http.server + sqlite3) HTTP app for the experiment coordinator: bearer-token-authed endpoints for claim arbitration, result/heartbeat/status/shutdown ingest, and writer-health reporting.
nodes.languageNotes: Zero third-party deps by design; ThreadingHTTPServer with a single BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass dispatching do_GET/do_POST.

nodes.name: db.py
nodes.summary: SQLite persistence layer for the coordinator: atomic claim arbitration (BEGIN IMMEDIATE + WAL), experiment upserts, heartbeats, results, command channel, and lifecycle-state derivation.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-process writer; claim atomicity relies on SQLite serialization + a conditional UPDATE WHERE clause instead of an external mutex.

nodes.name: manifest_spool.py
nodes.summary: Filesystem spool that persists POST /result manifest bytes (and companion side-files) to disk keyed on run_id so the Phase 3 git writer can later commit them into REE_assembly.

nodes.name: seed_from_queue.py
nodes.summary: CLI utility that seeds (or disaster-recovery reseeds) the coordinator DB from experiment_queue.json via idempotent upserts.

nodes.name: sync_daemon.py
nodes.summary: Sync daemon that in shadow mode reconciles the DB mirror to experiment_queue.json read-only, and in Phase-3 authoritative mode becomes the sole git writer committing result manifests, queue snapshots, and heartbeat telemetry.
nodes.languageNotes: Phase-3 writer body is feature-gated (SYNC_MODE=authoritative + --i-understand-phase3); three writer loops (git/queue/heartbeat) guard against dirty-tree and active-claim races.

nodes.name: test_manifest_spool.py
nodes.summary: Pytest suite covering the manifest spool round-trip (write/list/read/delete) and the Phase 3 writer guards.

nodes.name: test_phase3_claim_guard.py
nodes.summary: Offline smoke harness verifying sync_daemon._active_claim_blocks_writer and its three writer integration points block commits while an active claim covers a path.

nodes.name: test_phase3_command_channel.py
nodes.summary: Pytest suite for the Phase 3 coordinator command channel (insert/fetch/ack of per-machine runner commands).

nodes.name: test_phase3_heartbeat_writer.py
nodes.summary: Offline smoke harness for sync_daemon.phase3_heartbeat_writer, exercising heartbeat/status telemetry materialisation into git.

nodes.name: test_phase3_heartbeat_writer_state_change.py
nodes.summary: Contract tests for the 2026-05-31 state-change-triggered commit redesign of the heartbeat writer (commit only on state transitions, not every interval).

nodes.name: test_phase3_queue_add.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the Phase 3 POST /queue/add coordinator ingress where the DB owns the queue and experiment_queue.json is a derived view.

nodes.name: test_phase3_queue_writer.py
nodes.summary: Offline smoke harness for sync_daemon.phase3_queue_writer using a throwaway bare remote + working clone to verify queue-snapshot materialisation and push.

nodes.name: test_phase3_runpack_materialize.py
nodes.summary: Contract tests for Phase-3 git-writer run-pack materialisation (PHASE3_MATERIALIZE_RUNPACK), the complement to the flat-only silent-drop fix.

nodes.name: test_phase3_sidefile_sync.py
nodes.summary: Tests for Phase 3 companion side-file sync (episode logs and other artifacts emitted alongside a result manifest).

nodes.name: test_phase3_telemetry_dirt_recovery.py
nodes.summary: Tests for sync_daemon telemetry-only dirty-tree auto-recovery when the hub checkout is dirty solely under runner_heartbeats/ and runner_status/.

nodes.name: test_phase3_writer_health_bootstrap.py
nodes.summary: Contract tests for sync_daemon._bootstrap_writer_health_from_git, covering the four writer-health bootstrap contracts.

nodes.name: test_phase3_writer_smoke.py
nodes.summary: Offline smoke harness for sync_daemon.phase3_git_writer covering the cutover-gate cases from PHASE3_CUTOVER.md not exercised elsewhere.

nodes.name: test_shadow_e2e.py
nodes.summary: Local end-to-end self-test spinning the real coordinator entrypoint as a subprocess against a throwaway DB + tokens, no network or WireGuard.

nodes.name: test_shutdown_notify.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the shutdown_notify endpoint and lifecycle_state derivation across live/stale/shutdown transitions.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: Threaded HTTP request handler dispatching all coordinator endpoints (do_GET/do_POST) with bearer-token auth, JSON body parsing, and DB delegation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Process entry point that loads config, opens the DB, and starts the ThreadingHTTPServer.

nodes.name: auth_machine
nodes.summary: Validates an Authorization header value against the token map and returns the associated machine label or None.

nodes.name: _age_out_errors
nodes.summary: Filters aged-out entries from the writer-health error snapshot against a cutoff timestamp.

nodes.name: upsert_experiment
nodes.summary: Idempotently inserts or updates an experiment row from a queue item, optionally preserving an existing claim.

nodes.name: try_claim
nodes.summary: Atomically claims a queue item for a machine using BEGIN IMMEDIATE + a conditional UPDATE, respecting affinity, staleness, and fresh-owner heartbeats.

nodes.name: evaluate_claim
nodes.summary: Read-only evaluation of whether a machine could claim a queue item, used for git-vs-coordinator shadow verdict comparison.

nodes.name: release_claim
nodes.summary: Releases a machine's claim on a queue item, returning it to the unclaimed pool.

nodes.name: record_result
nodes.summary: Persists an experiment result row (outcome, manifest sha + byte count) keyed on run_id and queue_id.

nodes.name: upsert_heartbeat
nodes.summary: Inserts or updates a per-machine heartbeat row (state, current EXQ, progress, GPU, timing, raw payload).

nodes.name: record_shutdown_notice
nodes.summary: Records a machine shutdown notice with reason and expected wake condition for lifecycle-state derivation.

nodes.name: ack_command
nodes.summary: Acknowledges a pending runner command, recording its result status and note.

nodes.name: write_manifest
nodes.summary: Atomically spools raw manifest bytes plus metadata (relpath, received_at, sha256) to disk keyed on a sanitized run_id.

nodes.name: write_sidefile
nodes.summary: Spools a companion side-file artifact (e.g. episode log) alongside a run's manifest with path-safety checks.

nodes.name: derive_evidence_relpath
nodes.summary: Derives the REE_assembly evidence-tree relative path a manifest should be committed to from run_id and manifest content.

nodes.name: list_pending_run_ids
nodes.summary: Enumerates run_ids with pending spooled manifests awaiting the Phase 3 git writer.

nodes.name: delete_manifest
nodes.summary: Removes a spooled manifest and its metadata once the writer has committed it.

nodes.name: phase3_git_writer
nodes.summary: Phase-3 sole-git-writer loop that commits pending result manifests (and materialised run-packs/side-files) into REE_assembly and pushes, guarded against dirty-tree and active-claim races.

nodes.name: phase3_queue_writer
nodes.summary: Phase-3 writer loop that materialises experiment_queue.json from the DB and commits/pushes the snapshot, with conflict self-recovery.

nodes.name: phase3_heartbeat_writer
nodes.summary: Phase-3 writer loop that materialises per-machine heartbeat/status telemetry files and commits on state changes only.

nodes.name: reconcile_once
nodes.summary: Reads experiment_queue.json and reconciles the coordinator DB mirror to match it (shadow-mode, read-only on git).

nodes.name: _sync_to_origin
nodes.summary: Rebases writer-authored commits onto refreshed origin and pushes, absorbing non-conflicting operator races without autostash.

nodes.name: _active_claim_blocks_writer
nodes.summary: Checks whether an active TASK_CLAIMS entry covers any path a writer would touch, gating the commit to avoid clobbering session edits.

nodes.name: _materialize_runpacks
nodes.summary: Materialises per-run pack directories (runs/<run_id>/manifest.json) from spooled manifests to prevent flat-only silent drops.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Daemon entry point that parses flags, selects shadow vs authoritative mode, and drives the reconcile + writer loops.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that reads the queue JSON and upserts each item into the coordinator DB.

nodes.summary: Cross-stream decay regulator that registers streams and applies tonic exponential decay scaled by a shared GABA tone in the absence of above-threshold input.

nodes.name: InvalidationTrigger
nodes.summary: Broadcast invalidation trigger that steps per tick, tracks a tonic estimate, and re-emits boundary events as graded broadcast events for downstream reset consumers.

nodes.name: LPBInteroceptiveRouter
nodes.summary: Non-trainable router that masks external harm observations and builds a separable interoceptive harm channel from metabolic/visceral distress signals.

nodes.name: MECH295LikingBridge
nodes.summary: Bridge computing anticipatory liking write, approach-cue and conjunction score biases from drive_level per tick, feeding action selection.

nodes.name: SimulationModeRuleGate
nodes.summary: Categorical write gate that computes an effective simulation-mode tag and suppresses rule-arbitration weight updates while replay/simulation is active.

nodes.name: collapse_per_axis_drive
nodes.summary: Collapses a per-axis drive vector into a scalar effective drive using a validated per-consumer combiner (mean/max/etc.).

nodes.name: select_axis
nodes.summary: Routes a per-axis drive vector by axis index for axis-matched consumers such as the MECH-295 approach-cue case.

nodes.name: goal.py
nodes.summary: Superordinate goal representation subsystem: holds the persistent goal memory, incentive-token drive bank, and the live goal-state vector that drives cue-pull and proximity signals across the agent.

nodes.name: GoalConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass of hyperparameters for the goal subsystem (memory size, incentive decay, cue-pull gains, injection behaviour).

nodes.name: SuperOrdinalGoalMemory
nodes.summary: Slotted memory of superordinate goals supporting write, best-match retrieval, seed-noting and contextual-complexity queries.

nodes.name: IncentiveTokenBank
nodes.summary: Bank of decaying incentive tokens encoding wanting/drive per goal axis, with update, decay and most-wanted selection.

nodes.name: GoalState
nodes.summary: Live goal-state vector producing z_goal embedding, goal proximity/distance, cue-pull and injection dynamics used by predictors and the hippocampal module.

nodes.name: anchor_set.py
nodes.summary: Hippocampal anchor store: maintains spatial/goal anchors with dual-trace payloads, hysteresis, boundary-event consumption and goal-match queries.

nodes.name: AnchorGoalPayload
nodes.summary: Dataclass payload attached to an anchor carrying its associated goal embedding and dual-trace metadata.

nodes.name: Anchor
nodes.summary: Single hippocampal anchor with a goal-match scoring method against a query embedding.

nodes.name: AnchorSet
nodes.summary: Region-indexed collection of anchors supporting write, retrieval, inactivation, hysteresis ticking, boundary-event consumption and goal-match queries.

nodes.name: event_segmenter.py
nodes.summary: Multi-scale event boundary segmenter using prediction-error thresholding and Bayesian online changepoint detection to emit segment boundaries for the hippocampal module.

nodes.name: _PEThresholdDetector
nodes.summary: Prediction-error threshold boundary detector: steps on incoming PE signal and fires when it crosses an adaptive threshold.

nodes.name: _BOCPDGaussianDetector
nodes.summary: Bayesian online changepoint detector with a Gaussian predictive model producing run-length-based segment boundaries.

nodes.name: EventSegmenter
nodes.summary: Top-level segmenter that builds and drives per-scale detectors, tracks the current segment id and can force boundaries.

nodes.name: ghost_goal_bank.py
nodes.summary: Ghost-goal bank ranking dormant/abandoned goal anchors by persistence appraisal (staleness, recoverability, outshine gating, context salience) for possible reactivation.

nodes.name: GhostGoalBank
nodes.summary: Ranks ghost-goal anchors using staleness, recoverability, outshine gating and context salience, and exposes persistence-license diagnostics.

nodes.name: module.py
nodes.summary: Central hippocampal module orchestrating anchors, event segmentation, ghost-goal ranking, staleness, invalidation triggers and residue/predictor integration; the main memory/context hub of the architecture.
nodes.languageNotes: Very large single-class module (~2300 lines, 49 methods) acting as the hippocampal integration hub.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full hippocampal subsystem: boundary/broadcast event draining, action-object decoding, trajectory summarisation, anchor scaffolding and integration with segmenter, ghost-goal bank, staleness accumulator and residue field.

nodes.name: persistence_appraisal_compute.py
nodes.summary: Pure computation of an agent's persistence appraisal from goal activity, proximity, prior completion signal and E3 commitment state, feeding the ghost-goal bank.

nodes.name: compute_agent_persistence_appraisal
nodes.summary: Computes a persistence appraisal score/decision from goal-active, proximity, prior-completion and E3 commitment inputs.

nodes.name: staleness_accumulator.py
nodes.summary: Per-anchor staleness accumulator integrating and leaking staleness over time, with lookup, snapshot and decay operations used by the ghost-goal bank.

nodes.name: StalenessAccumulator
nodes.summary: Accumulates and leaks per-anchor staleness, supporting integrate, tick-leak, lookup-by-anchor-key, snapshot and partial decay.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Neuromodulation package entry point re-exporting the serotonin module.

nodes.name: serotonin.py
nodes.summary: Serotonin neuromodulator model governing tonic 5-HT level, sleep-phase transitions (SWS/REM), precision recalibration and seeding/wanting-floor gains.

nodes.name: SerotoninConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass of serotonin-module parameters (tonic gains, recalibration targets, seeding and wanting-floor settings).

nodes.name: SerotoninModule
nodes.summary: Serotonin dynamics: tracks tonic 5-HT and sleep phase, computes recalibration targets, benefit salience, seeding gain and wanting floor, and handles SWS/REM entry.

nodes.name: noisy_selection_head.py
nodes.summary: Noisy action-selection head adding gap-scaled stochastic perturbation to candidate scores for exploration during trajectory selection.

nodes.name: NoisySelectionHeadConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuring the noisy selection head (sigma scaling, gap sensitivity, activation).

nodes.name: NoisySelectionHead
nodes.summary: Selection head that scales injected noise by the observed score gap and applies it during forward selection, with reset and state export.

nodes.name: e2_fast.py
nodes.summary: E2 fast predictor: self and world forward models, contrastive world-forward losses, counterfactual rollouts and candidate/action generation over latent trajectories.

nodes.name: Trajectory
nodes.summary: Latent trajectory container exposing length, final state and state/world/action-object sequence accessors.

nodes.name: E2FastPredictor
nodes.summary: Fast predictor implementing self next-state prediction, world forward dynamics, contrastive multi-step losses, counterfactual and world-coupled rollouts and candidate generation.

nodes.name: e3_selector.py
nodes.summary: E3 trajectory selector: the large decision layer scoring candidate trajectories via harm/benefit evaluation, precision/commit-threshold dynamics, novelty and the noisy selection head; a central action-selection hub.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest module in the batch (~3500 lines, 41-method selector class) integrating goal, residue, E2 predictor and policy head.

nodes.name: project_channel_range
nodes.summary: Projects candidate feature channels into a normalised range for selection scoring.

nodes.name: E3TrajectorySelector
nodes.summary: Selects among candidate trajectories using harm/benefit evaluation, precision and commit-threshold control, running-variance/volatility estimation, novelty EMA and a noisy selection head.

nodes.name: field.py
nodes.summary: Residue field: RBF-based valence/affect memory over latent space with EWC anchoring, benefit and safety evaluation and accumulation, feeding the E3 selector and hippocampal module.

nodes.name: RBFLayer
nodes.summary: Radial-basis-function layer storing residue centres and valences with add, update and evaluate operations.

nodes.name: ResidueField
nodes.summary: Valence residue field with EWC anchoring/penalty, trajectory evaluation, and separate benefit and safety accumulation/evaluation channels.

nodes.name: replay_sampler.py
nodes.summary: Sleep replay sampler drawing anchors for offline replay from a frozen staleness/priority snapshot using softmax sampling, with region-count and uniformity diagnostics.

nodes.name: SleepReplaySampler
nodes.summary: Samples anchors for replay from a frozen snapshot via softmax over priorities, tracking draw counts, region counts and uniformity metrics.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central configuration hub: 16 nested dataclasses defining every subsystem's hyperparameters plus the top-level REEConfig with sizing presets and feature-flag toggles; imported across the codebase.
nodes.languageNotes: Very large config module (~6800 lines); REEConfig aggregates all subsystem configs and provides from_dims/large/xlarge presets.

nodes.name: REEConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level aggregate config composing all subsystem configs, with __post_init__ wiring, feature-flag toggles (sleep aggregation, goal stream) and from_dims/large/xlarge sizing presets.

nodes.name: E3Config
nodes.summary: Configuration for the E3 trajectory selector (precision, commit thresholds, harm/benefit, novelty, noisy head parameters).

nodes.name: HippocampalConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the hippocampal module and its sub-components (anchors, segmenter, staleness, ghost-goal bank).

nodes.name: LatentStackConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the latent representation stack dimensions and layers.

nodes.name: E1Config
nodes.summary: Configuration for the E1 encoder/predictor layer.

nodes.name: E2Config
nodes.summary: Configuration for the E2 fast predictor (world/self forward models, contrastive losses).

nodes.name: GhostGoalBankConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the ghost-goal bank ranking (persistence, staleness, outshine gating, salience).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the policy-layer package (action-commitment / basal-ganglia-eligibility modules), re-exporting the config and engine classes of every policy submodule. Anchors the ARC-062/063 rule-apprehension slot.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using explicit per-module re-exports and an __all__ list.

nodes.name: candidate_rule_field.py
nodes.summary: ARC-063 distributed CandidateRule field: a non-Bayesian, pure-arithmetic rule creator that mints distinct rules as pinned subspace slots and refines their availability via eligibility-trace credit, resolving the trainer-weights-rules-it-did-not-invent gap (MECH-309/598b).
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-arithmetic module (no nn.Module / trained parameters); differentiation is structural via subspace-partitioned slots.

nodes.name: commit_maintenance_release.py
nodes.summary: MECH-342 release-side complement to the MECH-090 commit-entry readiness conjunction: an OR-composed, bounded-accumulation release of an already-elevated beta latch when decisiveness or nav readiness degrade mid-commitment.

nodes.name: commit_readiness.py
nodes.summary: MECH-090 commit-entry readiness signal: a pure-arithmetic [0,1] motor-program readiness EMA (plus harness override seam) consumed by the readiness-above-floor leg of the R-c commit-entry conjunction in REEAgent.select_action.

nodes.name: difficulty_gated_proposal_entropy.py
nodes.summary: SD-061 / MECH-343 regulator mapping a stuck_score onto a transient proposal-layer gain (wider candidate set plus within-class sampling temperature), widening internal proposals under impasse without touching scoring or commitment thresholds.

nodes.name: gated_policy.py
nodes.summary: ARC-062 weak-reading rule-apprehension layer: two symmetry-broken scoring heads plus a learned context discriminator that gates between them, the V3-tractable instantiation of the MECH-309 behavioural-diversity slot.
nodes.languageNotes: The only trained module in this batch: PyTorch nn.Module heads/discriminator with a crystallize expansion path; siblings are pure-arithmetic regulators.

nodes.name: natural_commit_urgency.py
nodes.summary: Commit/release-duration lever (f_dominance_conversion_ceiling front, rung 6) that accumulates a graded natural-commit-occupancy urgency after commit entry, making the F-driven natural commit less monolithic so weak-natural-commit becomes the norm across seeds.

nodes.name: noise_floor.py
nodes.summary: MECH-313 stochastic noise floor (LC-NE tonic / SAC max-entropy analog): a state-independent scalar regulator that lifts the action-selection softmax temperature and enforces a hard minimum so the policy never collapses to argmax.

nodes.name: rho_maintenance_ramp.py
nodes.summary: ARC-108 rho_t maintenance ramp: a proximity-scaled driver that replaces the flat unconditional latch-hold of the MECH-090 commit latch, peaking then declining past the proximity peak so the commitment hold self-limits instead of running monolithically.

nodes.name: structured_curiosity.py
nodes.summary: MECH-314 structured curiosity bonus (frontopolar exploration / EFE analog): produces a per-candidate score bias on E3 scoring combining novelty, epistemic uncertainty, and learning-progress sub-flavours (MECH-314a/b/c).

nodes.name: tonic_vigor.py
nodes.summary: MECH-320 tonic vigor coupling: an additive, target-free vigor bias on E3 trajectory scoring proportional to a slow EWMA of realised score receipts, biasing toward action and away from no-op trajectories (mesolimbic-DA average-reward-rate analog).

nodes.name: CandidateRuleFieldConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the CandidateRule field: slot count, rule/context geometry, mint thresholds, tolerance/availability dynamics, and mature-pool maintenance parameters.

nodes.name: CandidateRule
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a single minted rule slot: pinned rule embedding, context tag, availability/eligibility scalars, mint/last-active timestamps, and its action-object index.

nodes.name: CandidateRuleField
nodes.summary: Core distributed rule field implementing the five faces (create/represent/gate/select/output+credit): mints rules on recurring context-action-outcome regularities, gates availability with a conflict-rising threshold, retrieves context-matched rules, and credits them via eligibility traces.

nodes.name: CommitMaintenanceReleaseConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the maintenance-release regulator: score-margin and nav readiness floors/reengage thresholds, accumulation/leak rates, and release/pressure bounds.

nodes.name: CommitMaintenanceRelease
nodes.summary: MECH-342 regulator that accumulates a bounded release pressure while an already-elevated beta latch runs, driven (OR-composed) by decaying decisiveness or nav readiness, and reports/reset that pressure to the commit-latch call site.

nodes.name: CommitReadinessConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the commit-readiness EMA: window, EMA alpha, and fail-open initial value.

nodes.name: CommitReadiness
nodes.summary: MECH-090 readiness regulator maintaining a [0,1] motor-program readiness EMA over per-tick outcome signals with an explicit harness override, exposing is_above_floor for the R-c commit-entry conjunction.

nodes.name: DifficultyGatedProposalEntropyConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the difficulty-gated proposal-entropy regulator: maximum extra candidates and maximum within-class temperature gain.

nodes.name: DifficultyGatedProposalEntropy
nodes.summary: SD-061 regulator mapping a stuck_score to a proposal-widening gain (extra candidates plus within-class temperature multiplier) that decays to the identity gain as the impasse clears; returns identity in simulation mode.

nodes.name: GatedPolicyConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the gated policy: head/discriminator dimensions and init scales, symmetry-breaking bias offsets, crystallize and differential-head options, and P1 auxiliary-loss weights.

nodes.name: GatedPolicyOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling a forward pass's outputs: the discriminator gating weight, the combined gated score bias, and the individual per-head biases.

nodes.name: GatedPolicy
nodes.summary: ARC-062 gated-policy nn.Module: two symmetry-broken scoring heads and a context discriminator that produces a gated per-candidate score bias, with a crystallize expansion path and a P1 training auxiliary loss enforcing mode separation.

nodes.name: NaturalCommitUrgencyReleaseConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the natural-commit-urgency release lever: urgency and action-extent modes, urgency rate/cap, release bound, gap-entry sensitivity, and onset ticks.

nodes.name: NaturalCommitUrgencyRelease
nodes.summary: Commit/release-duration regulator that arms on commit entry and accumulates a bounded natural-commit-occupancy urgency over ticks, shortening the F-driven natural-commit latch so weak-natural-commit becomes the cross-seed norm.

nodes.name: NoiseFloorConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the noise floor: the softmax-temperature lift alpha and the hard minimum effective temperature.

nodes.name: NoiseFloor
nodes.summary: MECH-313 pure scalar regulator computing an effective action-selection temperature as max(baseline + alpha, min_temperature), a state-independent floor preventing deterministic argmax collapse.

nodes.name: RhoMaintenanceRampConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for the rho_t maintenance ramp: hold floor, release margin, and onset grace ticks.

nodes.name: RhoMaintenanceRamp
nodes.summary: ARC-108 proximity-scaled maintenance driver that arms on commit entry and ramps the beta-latch hold up while approaching the goal then declines past the proximity peak, self-limiting the commitment hold by construction.

nodes.name: StructuredCuriosityConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for structured curiosity: enable flags and weights for the novelty, uncertainty, and learning-progress sub-flavours, bias scale, EMA/window parameters, novelty source, and spread thresholds.

nodes.name: StructuredCuriosity
nodes.summary: MECH-314 curiosity regulator computing a per-candidate E3 score bias from novelty (distance to active residue centers), epistemic uncertainty (running variance), and learning progress, tracking prediction error and candidate spread over time.

nodes.name: TonicVigorConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass for tonic vigor: EWMA half-life, action/passive weights, bias scale, energy/drive/prediction-error gate bounds, no-op class, and the v_t floor.

nodes.name: TonicVigorOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling a tonic-vigor computation: the v_t vigor level, max absolute bias, action/no-op candidate counts, and the energy/drive/prediction-error gate values.

nodes.name: TonicVigor
nodes.summary: MECH-320 tonic-vigor regulator maintaining a slow EWMA over negated E3 score receipts and emitting an additive, gated vigor bias toward action trajectories and away from no-ops at the e3.select() call site.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the sleep-aggregation cluster, re-exporting the phase manager, routing gate, Bayesian aggregator, MEL consumer, cross-module consolidator, self-model aggregator, replay sampler, and onset gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel; aggregates the MECH-272/273/275/285/286 sleep substrate into a single import surface.

nodes.name: bayesian_aggregator.py
nodes.summary: MECH-275 Phase D substrate: per-domain per-region Gaussian posterior over residuals, updated by conjugate mean-and-variance from probe-channel-routed replay events with snapshot-at-phase-switch and per-cycle decay.
nodes.languageNotes: Conjugate Gaussian update (precision-weighted); posteriors keyed on (domain, region) matching MECH-284 RegionKey.

nodes.name: cross_module_consolidation.py
nodes.summary: MECH-423 module-tagged interleaved offline consolidation orchestrator that runs a configurable E1/E2 schedule over replay traces, tagging each trace by which modules it updated to expose cross-module replay share for super-additivity acceptance checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure orchestration over caller-supplied loss closures; optimisers built locally over named modules only (MECH-094 weight-update exception).

nodes.name: mel_consumer.py
nodes.summary: SD-MEL-CONSUMER (INV-050 learning-demand sleep drive): reads accumulated waking Model Error Load and scales SWS/REM step counts (duration lever) plus optionally triggers cycle entry (timing lever) via a scale-free relative factor.
nodes.languageNotes: Relative (mel/ref) response with a tiny division-guard floor; distinct from and composable with the SD-037 arousal onset gate.

nodes.name: phase_manager.py
nodes.summary: SleepLoopManager and the canonical SleepPhase ordering: deterministic K-episode entry into the SD-017 sleep cycle, orchestrating WAKING->SWS->PHASE_SWITCH->REM->WRITEBACK and wiring the routing gate, aggregators, MEL consumer, and consolidator into each cycle.
nodes.languageNotes: TYPE_CHECKING-only imports of downstream substrate to preserve bit-identical OFF semantics; central hub of the sleep cluster.

nodes.name: routing_gate.py
nodes.summary: MECH-272 Phase C RoutingGate: state-conditioned per-phase anchor/probe channel weighting for replay events, emitting RoutedEvents whose non-negative channel weights downstream consumers multiply into write strength.
nodes.languageNotes: No trainable parameters; pure float arithmetic over a per-phase weight table with diagnostic counters.

nodes.name: self_model_aggregator.py
nodes.summary: MECH-273 Phase E SelfModelAggregator: subclass of BayesianAggregator specialised on the SD-003 causal_sig 'self' posterior that runs a bounded low-LR offline gradient pass on E2_harm_s using aggregator-corrected residuals as targets.
nodes.languageNotes: The single explicit MECH-094 exception: parameter-update gating opened only on E2_harm_s during the offline writeback pass.

nodes.name: sleep_onset_gate.py
nodes.summary: MECH-286 override-gated sleep-onset permit: sleep fires only when override_signal is low, max region staleness is high, and tonic threat is permissive, evaluated at episode boundary on waking state.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-condition joint permit with hyperarousal and narcolepsy lesion modes; master switch defaults OFF.

nodes.name: BayesianAggregator
nodes.summary: Maintains per-(domain, region) Gaussian posteriors over residuals, applying probe-weighted conjugate updates from replay events, exposing snapshot/decay for phase-switch isolation and diagnostic metrics.

nodes.name: BayesianAggregatorConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass of hyperparameters for the Bayesian aggregator (prior variance, likelihood variance, probe gain, decay factor, domains) with post-init validation.

nodes.name: CrossModuleConsolidator
nodes.summary: Runs a configurable interleaved/blocked multi-module offline consolidation schedule over replay draws, tagging traces by updated modules and reporting cross-module replay share and per-module readouts.

nodes.name: CrossModuleConsolidatorConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuring the consolidation schedule (steps, interleave mode, simulation_mode guard) with post-init validation.

nodes.name: MELConsumer
nodes.summary: Reads accumulated waking Model Error Load and computes a scale-free duration factor to scale SWS/REM step counts, plus an entry-permit check against a MEL threshold with a K-episode backstop.

nodes.name: WakingMELAccumulator
nodes.summary: Running-mean accumulator of per-step waking prediction error (e3), providing count, mean, and reset used to derive the wake-period MEL signal.

nodes.name: SleepLoopManager
nodes.summary: Tracks episodes-since-sleep and fires deterministic K-episode sleep cycles over the SD-017 REEAgent surface, running the routing gate, building evidence snapshots, and driving SWS/REM passes plus aggregator writeback.

nodes.name: RoutingGate
nodes.summary: Holds the current per-phase anchor/probe weight row and routes replay events into RoutedEvents (pure read, no mutation), tracking routed counts for diagnostics.

nodes.name: SelfModelAggregator
nodes.summary: BayesianAggregator specialisation for the 'self' domain that runs a bounded offline MSE gradient pass on E2_harm_s using posterior-mean residual targets drawn from the waking harm replay buffer.

nodes.name: evaluate_sleep_onset_permit
nodes.summary: Evaluates the MECH-286 three-condition sleep-onset permit (permissive override, sufficient staleness, permissive tonic threat) and returns whether sleep may fire plus a diagnostic breakdown.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the SD-035 amygdala analogue, re-exporting the BLA (basolateral) and CeA (central) analog modules and their config/output dataclasses. Documents the biological division of labour and the REEConfig.use_amygdala_analog master switch that gates both.

nodes.name: bla.py
nodes.summary: Basolateral-amygdala analogue (SD-035 / MECH-074a/b/d): a non-trainable arithmetic module that consumes the z_harm_a affective stream and emits arousal-dependent hippocampal encoding gain (inverted-U), a content-selective retrieval-bias vector, and a predictor-attribution-gated remap signal on harm prediction-error spikes.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-arithmetic PyTorch module (no gradient flow); maintains running mean/variance on harm-PE and a post-event decay window on encoding gain.

nodes.name: cea.py
nodes.summary: Central-amygdala analogue (SD-035 / MECH-046 / MECH-074c): a low-latency non-trainable module that projects z_harm_a to a low-frequency magnitude and, on threshold crossing, emits a pre-softmax mode-prior log-odds bias and a fast candidate-priming pulse into the SalienceCoordinator, with a cortical-confirmation override window.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-arithmetic scalar module; fast_prime pulse decays with a tau counter and can be overridden by cortical confirmation inside a bounded window.

nodes.name: BLAConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass of tunable BLA parameters: encoding-gain bounds and arousal thresholds, the post-event decay window, retrieval-bias alpha, and harm-PE remap thresholds (sigma, EMA alpha, code fraction, attribution gate).

nodes.name: BLAAnalog
nodes.summary: The BLA analogue engine: per-tick computes encoding gain, arousal tag, lazy retrieval-bias vector over hippocampal traces, and attribution-gated per-code remap amplitudes from harm prediction error, plus per-episode reset and diagnostics accessors.

nodes.name: CeAConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass of tunable CeA parameters: fast-route threshold, mode-prior log-odds bound and gain, pre-softmax additive term, fast-prime amplitude/decay/override-window steps, and cortical-confirmation weight.

nodes.name: CeAAnalog
nodes.summary: The CeA analogue engine: per-tick projects z_harm_a to a low-frequency magnitude, fires the fast threat gate to produce a bounded mode-prior bias and decaying fast-prime pulse into the SalienceCoordinator, tracks the cortical-confirmation override window, and exposes per-episode reset and diagnostics.

nodes.name: causal_grid_world.py
nodes.summary: CausalGridWorld V3 -- the core RL grid environment with split body_state/world_state observation channels (SD-005), effort-dissociation layouts, microhabitat zones, hazard/resource proximity fields, and multi-source weather/transient dynamics. Heavily imported substrate hub.
nodes.languageNotes: ~4500-line single-class env; CausalGridWorldV2 factory enables proxy-field (nociceptive/hedonic) observation mode.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld
nodes.summary: The grid-world environment class: reset/step dynamics, split observation encoding, effort costs, zone/hazard/resource fields, and rich telemetry.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorldV2
nodes.summary: Factory returning a CausalGridWorld configured in proxy-field mode (harm/benefit interoceptive + proximity-gradient exteroceptive channels).

nodes.name: cross_stream_binder.py
nodes.summary: Cross-stream binding factor that installs a genuine shared latent cause between the z_self and z_world rollout streams, injecting a joint-derived additive perturbation so cross-stream coherence carries per-candidate selection signal beyond prediction-error.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports FIXED (random-projection) and learned binder modes; preserves the byte-identical fixed path.

nodes.name: CrossStreamBinder
nodes.summary: Derives a shared factor from the joint (z_self,z_world) state, couples both streams with the same perturbation, and exposes binding-score/rebinding-probe diagnostics.

nodes.name: e2_world_uncertainty.py
nodes.summary: SD-063 conditional predictive-uncertainty head for the E2 world-forward model using distribution-free quantile (pinball) regression, giving E3's commit gate a state-conditional uncertainty signal instead of a global running-variance EMA.
nodes.languageNotes: Quantile-pinball head chosen over Gaussian forms per V3-EXQ-712; CRPS/precision-error-corr winner.

nodes.name: E2WorldUncertaintyConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuration for the E2 world-uncertainty head (quantile levels, dims, loss params).

nodes.name: E2WorldUncertaintyHead
nodes.summary: Quantile-regression head over (z_world,a) transitions producing a conditional predictive distribution with pinball loss and predictive std/variance readouts.

nodes.name: vs_rollout_gate.py
nodes.summary: MECH-269b symmetric V_s rollout gate: a consumer of per-stream V_s that substitutes the last-trusted snapshot at E1/E2 forward-predictor call sites when a stream's confidence drops below a per-side threshold, preventing rollouts off stale inputs.
nodes.languageNotes: Default no-op (V_s seeded at 1.0); fires only under MECH-287 failure-mode broadcasts.

nodes.name: VsRolloutGateConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuration for the V_s rollout gate, disabled by default for backward compatibility.

nodes.name: VsRolloutGate
nodes.summary: Per-stream snapshot store plus per-side threshold table that gates E1/E2 rollouts by substituting held snapshots when V_s falls low.

nodes.name: shared_latent_probe.py
nodes.summary: MECH-423 R1 readiness instrumentation: a pure function measuring per-module gradient coupling on a shared latent (non-zero grad norms + non-negative inter-module gradient cosine) to detect uncoupled or negative-transfer regimes.

nodes.name: shared_latent_gradient_probe
nodes.summary: Computes per-module gradient norms and mean pairwise cosine on a shared latent, flagging uncoupled or net-negative-transfer conditions.

nodes.name: version_layering.py
nodes.summary: Central registry and guard helpers for higher-version (V4/V5) architecture-epoch flags, enforcing that any V4/V5 substrate change to shared ree_core code defaults OFF so V3 execution stays bit-identical. Enumerates each generation-tagged master flag so parity smokes, contract tests, and the runner preflight can mechanically assert no-op-default safety.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a NamedTuple as an immutable flag record and dotted-path attribute walking (getattr chain) to probe a built REEConfig for drift; treats a missing path as a surfaced drift signal rather than a silent pass.

nodes.name: GenerationFlag
nodes.summary: Immutable NamedTuple describing one V4/V5 master gate on a shared code path: its generation, dotted config path, no-op default value, owning claim id, and a human note.

nodes.name: find_flags_on
nodes.summary: Returns the dotted paths of any registered V4/V5 flag not sitting at its no-op default on a given config, flagging missing paths as drift; an empty result means V3 default behaviour is preserved.

nodes.name: assert_all_off
nodes.summary: Raises AssertionError naming the offending flag paths if any registered V4/V5 flag is not at its no-op default, used by the runner preflight and no-op-default contract test to guard V3-default safety.

nodes.name: iter_generation_flags
nodes.summary: Returns the registered generation flags, optionally filtered to one or more given generations (e.g. v4, v5).

tour.title: What V3 Is Proving
tour.description: Start here to understand the project's mission and ethical framing before touching any code. This is the coordinator's plan-of-record, but read alongside the repo README it establishes the two standing gates (SENT-0: not claimed sentient; GOV-HEALTH-1: research substrate, not a clinical tool) and V3's core thesis: prerequisites for ethical testing (self/world separation, agency, harm modelling, commitment, residue, offline integration) must live in the state and trajectory itself, not in post-hoc safety judges. Keep this framing in mind as every downstream module turns out to be a piece of that argument.

tour.title: The Integration Spine
tour.description: REEAgent is the orchestrator that wires every brain-region-named subsystem into a single sense-plan-act loop; it has the highest fan-out in the codebase (imports ~43 modules), so it is the map of the whole architecture. There is no conventional main() -- experiments instantiate this agent against the grid-world environment and step it. Skim its imports now as a table of contents: the perceptual stack, E1/E2/E3 predictors, affect, hippocampal memory, policy, and sleep all appear here and each becomes a later stop on the tour.
tour.languageLesson: In Python, a top-level orchestrator like this typically composes subsystems by holding them as instance attributes rather than subclassing -- 'composition over inheritance'. Reading __init__ tells you the full dependency graph at a glance.

tour.title: Config and Goals: The Shared Vocabulary
tour.description: Before the subsystems, meet the two things they all share. REEConfig is the central hyperparameter and feature-flag hub -- 16 nested dataclasses defining every subsystem's knobs, imported across the whole codebase (fan-in 10), so feature flags here toggle entire mechanisms on and off. The goal subsystem holds the persistent goal memory, incentive-token drive bank, and the live goal-state vector that drives cue-pull and proximity signals -- the 'what the agent wants' that motivates action selection downstream.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses give you typed, defaulted config objects with almost no boilerplate. Nesting them (a dataclass field whose type is another dataclass) mirrors the subsystem hierarchy and makes feature flags discoverable by structure.

tour.title: The World and the Latent Stack
tour.description: This is where perception begins. CausalGridWorld is the RL environment, notable for splitting the observation into separate body_state and world_state channels (the SD-005 design) plus effort-dissociation layouts and hazard/resource proximity fields. The latent stack (L-space) is the encoder layer that mirrors that split, dividing the latent z_gamma into z_self and z_world streams -- the architectural root of the self/world separation that Step 1 named as a prerequisite for ethical testing.

tour.title: Predictive Core: E1, E2, E3
tour.description: The agent decides by predicting. E1 is the deep, slow LSTM world model predicting jointly over the concatenated self/world channels. E2 is the fast forward layer -- here the world-state model that doubles as a single-pass agency comparator (the prediction residual on the taken action IS the agency signal) and the sensory-discriminative harm model that forecasts damage. E3 is the trajectory selector, the large decision layer that scores candidate actions by harm/benefit, novelty, and commit-threshold dynamics -- the central action-selection hub everything else biases.
tour.languageLesson: The E1/E2/E3 naming encodes a timescale hierarchy (slow deep model, fast forward model, fast selector) -- a common pattern in predictive-processing architectures where each level predicts the level below and passes residuals upward.

tour.title: Affect and Defensive Valuation
tour.description: With harm predicted, the agent must feel and defend. The amygdala analogues turn the affective harm stream into control signals: the basolateral (BLA) module emits arousal-dependent memory-encoding gain and retrieval bias, while the central (CeA) module fires a fast pre-softmax mode-prior bias on threshold crossing. The PAG freeze-gate models freezing as a committed behavioural state with GABAergic commit/exit thresholds. These are mostly non-trainable arithmetic modules -- defensive reflexes wired into the state, exactly the 'harm-relevant primitives' the README flags as pre-ethical substrate.

tour.title: Residue, Suffering, and Blocked Agency
tour.description: Building on the affect signals from Step 6, these modules accumulate valence over time -- the slow, welfare-relevant state the project treats as a genuine research object. The residue field is an RBF-based valence memory over latent space with EWC anchoring, feeding both the E3 selector and the hippocampal module. The harm-suffering accumulator is a hysteretic integrator turning medium-timescale unpleasantness into a controllability-gated suffering load; the blocked-agency stream rises when an intended action repeatedly fails against an external constraint while the goal and capacity-belief are retained.
tour.languageLesson: Hysteresis (separate rise and fall thresholds so a state 'latches') recurs across these modules -- it is how the architecture models states like suffering or freezing that persist beyond their triggering stimulus rather than tracking it instantaneously.

tour.title: Memory and Context: The Hippocampal Hub
tour.description: The hippocampal module is the main memory/context hub, orchestrating anchors, event segmentation, ghost-goal ranking, staleness, and invalidation, and integrating the residue and predictor signals from earlier steps. Its parts (each high fan-in in the graph): the anchor store maintains spatial/goal anchors with dual-trace payloads and hysteresis; the ghost-goal bank ranks dormant or abandoned goals for possible reactivation; the staleness accumulator integrates and leaks staleness per anchor, feeding that ghost-goal ranking. Together they let the agent hold, forget, and revive goals across time.

tour.title: Action Commitment and Policy
tour.description: Perception, affect, and memory converge on the choice of what to do -- and crucially, whether to stick with it. The gated policy is the rule-apprehension layer (two symmetry-broken scoring heads plus a learned context discriminator that gates between them). Commit-readiness is a pure-arithmetic motor-program readiness signal feeding the commit-entry conjunction, and its release-side complement lets an already-latched commitment dissolve when readiness degrades mid-action. The actor-critic is the first-class learning substrate: a dorsal-striatal policy actor plus a value critic taught by reward-prediction-error. This commitment gating is another named ethical-testing prerequisite.

tour.title: Regulation and Neuromodulation
tour.description: Cross-cutting neuromodulatory control tunes everything above rather than living in the sense-plan-act path. The cingulate salience coordinator binds five cingulate subdivisions into a soft operating-mode probability vector with a hysteresis-gated mode-switch, driving write-gates. Serotonin governs tonic 5-HT level, sleep-phase transitions, and precision recalibration. The V_s rollout gate substitutes a last-trusted snapshot at predictor call sites when a stream's confidence drops, preventing rollouts off stale internal state, while the GABAergic decay regulator applies broad tonic inhibition across registered streams.

tour.title: Offline Consolidation: Sleep
tour.description: The last piece of the cognitive architecture is the offline-integration prerequisite from Step 1. The SleepLoopManager orchestrates a deterministic K-episode cycle (WAKING -> SWS -> PHASE_SWITCH -> REM -> WRITEBACK), wiring the routing gate, aggregators, and consolidation. The replay sampler draws anchors for offline replay from a frozen staleness/priority snapshot; the routing gate weights per-phase anchor/probe channels; and cross-module consolidation runs a tagged E1/E2 schedule over replay traces to expose cross-module replay share. This is how the agent integrates experience when it is not acting.

tour.title: Version-Layering Guardrails
tour.description: A short but load-bearing stop that explains how the substrate evolves safely. This registry enforces that any higher-version (V4/V5) architecture-epoch flag defaults OFF, so every V4/V5 change to shared ree_core code leaves V3 execution bit-identical. This is why the config from Step 3 is riddled with feature flags: new mechanisms land dark and are proven behind a flag before they alter the running substrate -- the mechanism behind the README's 'maintained claim, re-asserted each generation boundary' framing.

tour.title: The Experiment-Dispatch Plane
tour.description: Everything so far is the agent under test; this final arc is the machinery that runs experiments against it at fleet scale. The coordinator app is a pure-stdlib HTTP service (http.server + sqlite3) exposing bearer-token endpoints for claim arbitration and result/heartbeat/status ingest. The DB layer (highest fan-in in the whole graph, 17) is the SQLite persistence core doing atomic claim arbitration with BEGIN IMMEDIATE + WAL. Its schema defines the experiments queue, results, telemetry, and claim-log audit tables -- the shared state that lets a worker fleet run these experiments without duplicating work.
tour.languageLesson: SQLite's BEGIN IMMEDIATE plus WAL mode gives the coordinator an atomic compare-and-set for claims: the first worker to commit wins the row, and losers get a conflict rather than a duplicate run -- a database-level mutex with no external message broker.

tour.title: Git as the Sync Backbone
tour.description: The capstone shows how experiment results flow back to the research record. The sync daemon runs in two modes: a read-only shadow that reconciles the DB mirror against experiment_queue.json, and a Phase-3 authoritative mode where it becomes the sole git writer, committing result manifests, queue snapshots, and heartbeat telemetry into the REE_assembly repo. Understanding this closes the loop from Step 1: experiments defined in the plan-of-record run on the fleet (Step 13) and land as durable, governance-ready evidence here.
